channel.title: My Portrait
channel.description: Updates for My Portrait — a private, on-device AI memory system for macOS.

item.description:
Features

  • New on-device Qwen3-ASR transcription engine — better Chinese, no extra setup. Download models on demand.
  • Faster & lighter — removed the memory-heavy semantic search; search now runs on FTS5.
  • Pick your microphone (or follow the system default) with auto-fallback when devices unplug, plus a live "recording from" indicator.
  • Reliable system-audio capture — records the other side of calls and what you hear, even after switching output devices.
  • Pause capture for chosen apps or categories while they're playing audio (configurable, not just music).
  • Voice ID, reworked — train your voice (and others), listen to a clip of any detected voice, and auto-organize clusters. Trained speakers are searchable as @speaker:<name> in chat.
  • Auto-organize events into project folders (Event classifier) on a schedule.
  • Import conversations from Claude Code CLI and Codex CLI.
  • Manage scheduled jobs from chat — create, list, and delete them; live streaming; per-job mute; drag-and-drop & paste images.
  • Health & diagnostics — stall detection in the menu bar + one-click diagnostic export.
  • Multi-language transcription picker and "only transcribe while plugged in".

Bug fixes

  • System audio no longer goes silent shortly after recording starts.
  • Fixed an occasional app freeze caused by a database deadlock.
  • ChatGPT login no longer breaks under concurrent token refresh.
  • Crash fixes in the audio-capture restart path and typing capture.
  • Writing capture is more verbatim and accurate.
  • Light-theme visibility fixes across chat and connection views.
  • Removed a batch of dead or misleading settings.
  • Privacy controls: ignore incognito windows, record while screen is locked, and Power mode.

Features

  • Browser & canvas writing capture — properly captures what you write in Safari/Chrome/Arc and canvas editors (Google Docs, Notion), reconstructing edit history to match the document's own version history.
  • Ask the AI about what you wrote — chat now answers from your distilled writing records, and search returns the exact matching frame with a snippet.
  • Paste & drag-and-drop images and files straight into the chat input.
  • Daily scheduled jobs catch up — if the app was closed at a job's scheduled time, it runs on next launch instead of waiting another 24 hours.

Bug fixes

  • Toggling system audio off no longer crashes the app.
  • Writing capture is more accurate — keystroke-gated, attributed per browser tab, with far fewer false positives.

Features

  • Chat now knows what you've been doing — ask "summarize my yesterday" or "what was I writing in Cursor" and it answers from your real screen activity, all queried on-device.
  • Mode-aware Quick Actions — the suggestion cards adapt to whether you've been coding, browsing, in a meeting, or writing, and send the relevant context along.
  • Pre-downloaded transcription models — all 4 Whisper sizes ship on first launch, so switching size is instant.
  • Local capture models card shows ready / downloading status for each on-device model.

Bug fixes

  • Voice training works — fixed three crashes and two correctness bugs; re-training a name now replaces the old samples.
  • Time accounting now matches macOS Screen Time (was off by ~7×).
  • Chat input grows to 4 lines then scrolls; hovering a message no longer shifts it, and copy/edit/regenerate work from anywhere on the bubble.
  • Light-theme fixes for the timeline toolbar/calendar and the Memory/Input middle column.

Bug fixes

  • Voice training no longer crashes when you click "Start training".
  • The Memory page's middle column now matches the Light theme (no more out-of-place dark grey panel).

Bug fixes

  • Light theme: chat input, the @ / shield / paperclip icons, and the setup banner are readable again (were white-on-white).
  • Light theme: timeline calendar dates, month switcher and selected-day highlight are visible again.
  • Light theme: Connections buttons, the Memory search bar, and Settings tab icons / range chips now adapt correctly.

Features

  • Voice training, rebuilt — fully decoupled from transcription (record 30s on-device, no power needed); re-training replaces the old samples; it pre-checks the voice model is downloaded before recording.
  • Cleaner writing capture — strips window chrome, far fewer duplicates, no truncation of long writing, better per-record summaries, and keystroke-gated so it's signal-only.
  • Local capture models card in Settings → AI models shows ready / downloading status for each on-device model.

Bug fixes

  • Toggling system audio no longer crashes on an audio-format mismatch.
  • Notification overlay: Light-theme colors fixed, and the panel height now matches its content.

Features

  • Fully silent auto-update — new versions download in the background, show a small "click to postpone" banner, and install on their own after 10 seconds.
  • The update-check interval now respects your setting, and "Check now" always gives an explicit yes/no.
  • The installed version now shows in Settings → General and the menu-bar dropdown.

Bug fixes

  • Notification overlay panel resized to a sane height.

Features

  • Import your existing Screenpipe data — auto-scans disks and imports MP4 / audio metadata, with a progress bar.
  • Local models pre-fetch on launch, so the first run no longer stalls.
  • Proper Light theme — warm-white → lavender gradient with dynamic glass.
  • Custom dock icon, auto-cropped to the macOS squircle.
  • Speakers folded into the Audio Capture page.

Bug fixes

  • Chat: provider/model locked per conversation; RECENTS ordering fixed; empty placeholder hidden.
  • Onboarding no longer proactively requests permissions; window-resize fix.
  • Scheduler: 24-hour time picker with aligned controls.
  • Connections: brand icons for DeepSeek / Ollama / Anthropic / Obsidian / Notion; Claude Code "Connect" actually verifies the link.
  • Cron jobs: trimmed conversations no longer leak into RECENTS.
  • Sidebar: Memories rows fully clickable; translucent-sidebar toggle takes effect.
  • Assorted icon, layout, and Swift 6 data-race fixes.

⚠️ Upgrading from 1.0.0: install this .dmg manually and re-grant Screen Recording / Microphone / Accessibility / Input Monitoring / Full Disk Access — the code-signing identity changed, so this one update can't go through Sparkle and macOS treats it as a new app. Auto-updates work normally after 1.0.81.
• First release — see the GitHub release page for full notes.
